release_scope: Unreleased aoa-evals strategic refactor surfaces through the receipt-intake dry review slice: root design, agent route mesh, decisions, roadmap, quests, proof topology, legacy naming, mechanics packages, generated readers, proof-loop reports, receipt-intake dry review, validators, and tests.

requirements_review.claim_limit: Design and route surfaces are present and validator-covered for local release-prep review; this does not replace bundle-local proof meaning or close the strategic goal.
Decision memory covers the strategic topology route through the latest dry-review decision; decisions explain rationale and do not become source proof authority.
Quest routes and generated quest readers are release-reviewable as obligation surfaces; they do not promote quests into eval bundles or close the long refactor.
Topology, legacy, and mechanics surfaces make authority classes reviewable; they do not move source proof meaning out of bundles.

The active proof loop has smoke, bundle-local report, generated reader, and non-publishing receipt-intake dry review; it still does not publish a receipt, promote a bundle, or complete the whole goal.
Generated readers are checkable release inputs; they route readers back to sources and are not proof authority.
Local release gate coverage constrains current route invariants; green checks do not replace GitHub Repo Validation, reviewer judgment, or bundle-local review.
Sibling refs are routed as compatibility evidence only; this audit does not mutate sibling repos or transfer sibling authority into aoa-evals.

verification_snapshot.claim_limit: Validates repository invariants for the current local checkout; does not prove GitHub Repo Validation for an unmerged PR.
Checks shaped semantic AGENTS docs; does not prove every future agent route is complete.
Checks nested route-card coverage; does not prove source proof meaning.
Checks generated catalog freshness; generated output remains subordinate to source bundles.
Checks report index freshness; the report index is not receipt or verdict authority.
Checks runtime candidate template index freshness; runtime candidates remain candidate evidence.
Checks runtime candidate intake freshness; intake does not accept runtime verdict authority.
Checks phase-alpha matrix freshness; phase-alpha naming remains legacy or projection posture where applicable.
Latest-sibling canary was local compatibility evidence; it is not sibling release approval.
Full local tests passed for this checkout; tests do not prove the strategic goal is complete.
Local release audit passed; it is not a tag, GitHub release, PR approval, or proof that eval claims grew stronger.
Whitespace diff hygiene passed; it does not prove semantic completion.

publication_boundary.boundary: This readiness audit is not a release, not a tag, not GitHub Repo Validation, not a GitHub Release, not PR approval, not an eval result receipt, and not goal completion.

open_requirements_before_publication: review the accumulated diff as one bounded release-prep scope
choose release or PR title and version posture without strengthening eval claims
commit the intended diff on a branch based on current origin/main
open a PR that names changed surfaces, validation, skipped checks, and remaining risk
observe GitHub Repo Validation for the PR
merge only after required checks are green
create any tag or GitHub Release only after the release-prep change lands on main
perform a separate requirement-by-requirement goal completion audit before marking the long goal complete
claim_limit: This audit proves local release-prep reviewability for the accumulated strategic refactor diff. It does not publish a release, create a tag, open or approve a PR, observe GitHub Repo Validation, publish an eval result receipt, promote any bundle, accept runtime evidence, mutate sibling repos, or mark the aoa-evals strategic goal complete.
